options.description: Sets the height of the containing element, can be set to any valid height css value. If set to false (the default), the height of the table will resize to fit the table data.

enum.description: Only render rows that are visible in the viewport (default). Recommended for large datasets — dramatically improves performance.
Render all rows at once. Use for small datasets or when virtual rendering causes issues with certain CSS layouts.

options.description: Manually set the size of the vertical renderer buffer
Set the tables horizontal renderer

enum.description: Render all columns at once (default). Suitable for most use cases.
Only render columns visible in the viewport. Use for tables with a very large number of columns to improve performance.

enum.description: Each column width is set to fit its data. A horizontal scrollbar appears if all columns exceed the table width (default).
Columns are sized to fill the full width of the table. All column widths are equalised.
Same as fitData but the last column expands to fill any remaining space.
Same as fitData but all columns are stretched equally to fill the table width.
Same as fitData but the table element itself resizes to match the total column width (no horizontal scrollbar).
